subtitle: Public evidence and accountability interface for a climate-triggered malaria service-delivery Proof of Concept in flood-prone Bayelsa communities.
organisation: Hensard University · Toru-Orua, Bayelsa State, Nigeria

claims.text: Three live Google Earth Engine applications demonstrate technical readiness for rainfall warning, malaria-prone days and Sentinel-1 flood detection.
A published Bayelsa healthcare-accessibility baseline provides the starting evidence for settlement and facility-catchment planning.
The redesigned public database uses settlements and health-facility catchments as the operational units and keeps government, modelled and surveyed evidence separate.
Pilot catchments, local action thresholds, route states, facility readiness and target-population denominators still require government confirmation and PoC field validation.
The portal does not yet demonstrate improved malaria-service continuity, clinical outcomes or cost-effectiveness; these are the outcomes to be tested.
